en.extract:
The olive-winged bulbul (Pycnonotus plumosus) is a member of the bulbul family of passerine birds. 
It is found in south-eastern Asia and the Greater Sunda Islands. 
Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.


Diet
It eats fruit and insects.


Taxonomy and systematics
Until 2010, the ashy-fronted bulbul was also considered as a subspecies of the olive-winged bulbul.


Subspecies
Four subspecies are recognized:

P. p. porphyreus - Oberholser, 1912: Found on western Sumatra and nearby islands
P. p. plumosus - Blyth, 1845: Found on Malay Peninsula, eastern Sumatra, Java, Bali and western and southern Borneo
P. p. hutzi - Stresemann, 1938: Found on northern and eastern Borneo
P. p. hachisukae - Deignan, 1952: Found on islands off northern Borneo and south-western Philippines


References

The '''olive-winged bulbul''' ('''''Pycnonotus plumosus''''') is a member of the [[bulbul]] family of [[passerine]] [[bird]]s. 
It is found in south-eastern Asia and the [[Greater Sunda Islands]]. 
Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s.

== Diet ==
It eats fruit and insects.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=MOKHTER |first1=NORMAISHARAH |last2=AKHSAN |first2=MUHAMMAD ARIFUDDIN |last3=AMRAN |first3=MUHAMMAD ASRAF |last4=TZE |first4=JIEN LEE |last5=ZAINAL |first5=MD-ZAIRI |last6=ABDUL-LATIFF |first6=MUHAMMAD ABU BAKAR |last7=NORAZLIMI |first7=NOR ATIQAH |date=2022-11-30 |title=Feeding Ecology of Birds in Selected Microhabitat in Pulau Tinggi, Johor, Malaysia |journal=Journal of Sustainability Science and Management |volume=17 |issue=11 |pages=68–80 |doi=10.46754/jssm.2022.11.008 |issn=1823-8556}}</ref>

==Taxonomy and systematics==
Until 2010, the [[ashy-fronted bulbul]] was also considered as a subspecies of the olive-winged bulbul.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/archives/species-2-0-2-11/|title=Species Version 2 « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-03-31}}</ref>

===Subspecies===
Four [[subspecies]] are recognized:<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/bulbuls/|title=Bulbuls « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-03-30}}</ref>
* ''P. p. porphyreus'' - <small>[[Harry C. Oberholser|Oberholser]], 1912</small>: Found on western [[Sumatra]] and nearby islands 
* ''P. p. plumosus'' - <small>[[Edward Blyth|Blyth]], 1845</small>: Found on [[Malay Peninsula]], eastern Sumatra, [[Java]], [[Bali]] and western and southern [[Borneo]]
* ''P. p. hutzi'' - <small>[[Erwin Stresemann|Stresemann]], 1938</small>: Found on northern and eastern Borneo
* ''P. p. hachisukae'' - <small>[[Herbert Girton Deignan|Deignan]], 1952</small>: Found on islands off northern Borneo and south-western Philippines
